## Resolution

Dropped the field and the column; renumbered the cable `display` splits (10→9, 7→6, 11→10) and the header-comment column map.

body:
## Resolution
Every source render paid `git status` + `rev-parse` + `rev-list` per cloned repo plus a `read < <(...)` fork per row (226 rows). Rewrote `git_list_status_fields` around a single `git status --porcelain=v2 --branch` call parsed in-process, returning via `GIT_MARKER`/`GIT_SUMMARY` globals. Warm `list all` measured 0.98 s → 0.50 s. During verification this surfaced a real parse bug — the script-global `IFS=$'\n\t'` left `# branch.ab +0 -0` unsplit (space-separated) — fixed with a scoped `IFS=' '` on that read. Also verified end-to-end: 28 clones detected, markers ✓/●/⇡/? correct (a first diagnostic falsely showed all-○ because macOS UTF-8 collation makes `sort`/`uniq`/awk `==` treat ○/✓/● as equal; byte-exact `LC_ALL=C` counting confirmed correctness).

body:
## Resolution
`catppuccin/tmux` `status_module.conf` uses `set -ogqF` (only-if-not-set) for `text_fg` and `icon_fg`. On a dark→light switch, Mocha's `text_fg` (`#cdd6f4`, near-white) persisted because the palette files only unset those variables for `directory`, `session`, `application`, and `date_time` modules — not `battery`. This left near-white text over the transparent status background (terminal white in Latte), making battery text invisible. The reverse (light→dark) had the same bug: Latte's dark `text_fg` (`#4c4f69`) persisted in Mocha, rendering dark text on a dark terminal background. Fixed by adding `set -Ugq @catppuccin_status_battery_text_fg` and `set -Ugq @catppuccin_status_battery_icon_fg` to the unset block in both `config/theme/palettes.d/tmux.light.conf` and `config/theme/palettes.d/tmux.dark.conf`, matching the existing pattern for the other four modules.

body:
## Resolution
`_dfm_selfdir` was `dirname "$0"` with no symlink resolution. Invoked via a prior
install's `~/.local/bin/dfm` symlink, `$0` is the symlink path, so `_dfm_selfdir` became
`~/.local/bin` — which need not contain newly added dfm-* siblings (or even dfm-lib) until
`./install` links them, and with the repo's local/bin absent from $PATH the dispatcher failed
to load dfm-lib at all (died at the N-133 guard) and could not find new subcommands. This made
developing a new `dfm-<sub>` require a re-link first. Fixed by resolving `$0` through its
symlink chain (portable `readlink` loop, no `-f`, with `cd -P`) so `_dfm_selfdir` is the
script's REAL directory (the repo's local/bin), keeping the script's own folder authoritative
ahead of $PATH. Verified: a fresh repo-only `dfm-zzdemo`, never linked and with repo/local/bin
off $PATH, runs via a symlinked `dfm zzdemo`; `dfm help`/`check arch` via symlink still work;
43/43 bats pass; `yarn lint` clean.

### Pass 24 — 2026-06-03

Migrated from v1 `nitpicker-findings.md` (Pass 44, 2026-07-06).
title: dfm dispatcher resolves its dir from the symlink path, missing repo-side dfm-* before ./install

body:
## Resolution
After the dfm command split, the dispatcher's `$PATH` fallback was
`if command -v "dfm-$sub"; then exec "dfm-$sub"`. bash's `command -v` returns a
non-executable file when it is first on `$PATH`, and `$DOTFILES/local/bin` (which holds the
0644 `dfm-lib`) is on `$PATH` via `config/exports:25`. So `dfm lib` resolved `dfm-lib` and
exec-ed it → "Permission denied", nonzero exit — defeating the design property that the
non-executable dfm-lib is undispatchable. Fixed by resolving the path and guarding the exec
with `-x`: `resolved=$(command -v "dfm-$sub"); [[ -n "$resolved" && -x "$resolved" ]] && exec`.
Verified: `dfm lib` now falls through to usage, exit 0, no "Permission denied".

Migrated from v1 `nitpicker-findings.md` (Pass 44, 2026-07-06).
title: dfm dispatcher exec-s the non-executable dfm-lib via the $PATH fallback

body:
## Resolution
`source "$_dfm_selfdir/dfm-lib"` was unguarded; a missing/broken dfm-lib left
`dfm_bootstrap`/`msgr` undefined and `$SCRIPT` empty ("Usage:  <section>") yet the dispatcher
still returned 0, masking a broken install. Guarded with
`if ! source "$_dfm_selfdir/dfm-lib"; then echo "dfm: cannot load … (broken install?)" >&2;
exit 1; fi`. Verified: missing dfm-lib now exits 1 with a clear message.

The dispatcher called `dfm_bootstrap` at top level (sourcing `shared.sh` + `msgr`) and
then exec-ed a subcommand that bootstrapped again — twice the work for every `dfm <section>`,

`usage()` (the only path that needs `$SCRIPT`/`msgr`); the dispatch path now execs without
bootstrapping, so `shared.sh` is sourced once, inside the chosen subcommand. Verified: 43/43
bats pass; `dfm_bootstrap` now appears only inside `usage()`.

## Resolution
A regex like `^[[:space:]]*rg\b` matches `rg foo` but misses `git status |
grep foo`, `echo $(rg pattern)`, `false || cat file`, and similar. The hook splits
the command on pipeline separators (`|`, `&&`, `||`, `;`) and command substitutions
(`$( )`, backticks) using awk, then checks each segment's first word.

body:
## Resolution
`strip_env_prefix` originally only handled `env VAR=val cmd`. Bash also
permits `VAR=val cmd` and `A=1 B=2 cmd` without `env`. First-word check on `FOO=bar`
isn't on any list → fell through to allow. Extended the strip regex to handle both
forms with and without the `env` keyword.

## Resolution

to allow. Added them all to the deny list so subshell wrappers can't hide denied
commands inside their `-c` argument or heredoc body. `no-hook-bypass.md` now also
explicitly forbids this pattern.

title: `bash -c '<denied>'` and `bash <<EOF<denied>EOF` heredocs bypassed routing

body:
## Resolution
`env grep foo` (just `env` + command, no assignments) → strip regex required
`+` (one or more VAR=val) → didn't strip → first_word `env` was unknown → allowed.
Changed the env-strip regex from `+` to `*` so `env` alone also peels off.

title: `env cmd` (without VAR=val tokens) bypassed routing

body:
## Resolution
The marker check was `(^|[[:space:]])BASH_OK([[:space:]]|$)` — matched
`BASH_OK` as a word anywhere, including `rg BASH_OK src/` (an accidental bypass if
the user grepped for a symbol literally called BASH_OK). Tightened to
`^[[:space:]]*BASH_OK[[:space:]]+` so only a leading marker triggers the escape.

body:
## Resolution
`config/mise/config.toml` contains 10+ `"github:..."` tools. mise resolves `"latest"`
by querying the GitHub API — one request per `github:` tool. Unauthenticated requests are
rate-limited to 60/hr; on a fresh machine install, this exhausts the budget mid-run and
leaves tools partially installed with no clear error. Added a check for `${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}` in
the `mise)` case before calling `mise install --yes`. If unset, warns about rate limits and
offers to run `dfm secrets github` interactively, sourcing the resulting file so the token
is available for the current process before continuing.

body:
## Resolution
Fish sources `config/fish/secrets.d/*.fish` at shell startup (via `exports.fish`).
Bash and zsh had no equivalent — only `exports-secret` and `exports-local` single-file
fallbacks. No dfm command existed to scaffold secrets files across all shells. Added:
(1) `section_secrets()` function in dfm with a `github` subcommand that prompts for
GITHUB_TOKEN (silent input via `read -rsp`) and writes fish and bash/zsh secrets files;
(2) `config/secrets.d/github.sh.example` template parallel to the fish example;
(3) `config/secrets.d/*` + allowlist entries in `.gitignore` so real secrets stay untracked;
(4) secrets.d sourcing loop at the end of `config/exports` for bash/zsh, matching the
existing fish pattern exactly.

body:
## Resolution
The usage CLI v3+ supports inline `#USAGE` comment directives embedded directly in
scripts, eliminating the need for paired `.kdl` sidecar files. 18 sidecar files under
`scripts/` and 56 under `local/bin/` were replaced by injecting `#USAGE about "..."` (and
`flag`/`arg`/`cmd`/`alias` blocks where the KDL carried them) immediately after the shebang
and `# @description` line in each script. PHP scripts use `//USAGE` (PHP line-comment
prefix). All 74 KDL files were deleted. The `# @description` tag was preserved in all scripts
that carried it — it is consumed by `dfm get_script_description()` independently of usage.

title: `scripts/` and `local/bin/` usage specs were sidecar `.kdl` files, not inline

body:
## Resolution
The completion generator looped over `**/*.usage.kdl` globs and passed the KDL sidecar
path to `usage generate -f`. After the migration to inline specs, no `.kdl` files exist and
the script generated nothing. Refactored into a `generate_for_spec(spec, bin_name)` helper
function that runs the five generators (fish/bash/zsh completions, markdown, manpage). Two
replacement loops: `local/bin/` uses `grep -q '#USAGE\|//USAGE'` as a self-filter so only
scripts with inline directives are processed; `scripts/*.sh` iterates all `.sh` files,
skipping `shared.sh` (library). `usage generate -f` accepts inline-annotated scripts directly.

users to run `dfm docs script <name>`, which is the old pre-migration workflow. Added
`#USAGE about "<one-line description>"` immediately after `# @description` in the template,
added bullet points explaining both tags' purposes, and replaced Step 3 with
`scripts/install-completions.sh` as the correct regen entry point.

body:
## Resolution
Both the Read block (line 18) and Edit/Write block (line 43) contained the
exception `*.example.fish | *.fish.example`. The `*.example.fish` half matches any
file ending in `.example.fish` — e.g., `prod.example.fish` in `secrets.d/`, which
fish auto-sources as a `*.fish` file and which `.gitignore` treats as a secret. That
file would pass the exception and be readable/editable despite containing credentials.
The tracked example naming convention is `*.fish.example` only (e.g.,
`github.fish.example`). Removed `*.example.fish |` from both case branches so only
`*.fish.example` files are exempted. Copilot report PR #376 r3262054215.

body:
## Resolution
With `set -euo pipefail`, a non-zero exit from
`php composer-setup.php --quiet` aborted the script before reaching the
cleanup `rm composer-setup.php` and the `mv composer.phar` lines. Net result:
`composer-setup.php` and possibly a partial `composer.phar` were left in the
caller's cwd. The `RESULT=$?` capture and the `if [[ $RESULT -eq 0 ]]; then mv...`
branch was also dead code under `set -e` — `RESULT` could only ever be 0 by
the time the if ran. Rewrote to `cd "$(mktemp -d)"` with `trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT`
and dropped the dead `RESULT` plumbing. Also added `mkdir -p ~/.local/bin`
so the final `mv` doesn't fail on a fresh box.

Migrated from v1 `nitpicker-findings.md` (Pass 44, 2026-07-06).
title: `install-composer.sh` leaked files in cwd on installer failure under `set -e`

body:

success path or on openssl failure. SIGINT / SIGTERM / SIGHUP between the
mktemp and the `rm -f` left the temp file under `/tmp` (or `$TMPDIR`).
Added `trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT INT TERM HUP` after mktemp and a
`trap - EXIT INT TERM HUP` after the explicit rm so the trap doesn't
re-fire on the next loop iteration's mktemp.

body:
## Resolution
`config/nvim/lua/utils.lua` had
`if not desc.desc then desc.desc = '?'; return desc end` — mutating the
caller's table. Concrete failing scenario: a user passes a shared opts
table to `K.n('a', cmdA, opts)` then `K.n('b', cmdB, opts)`; after the
first call `opts.desc == '?'`, so the second call sees a populated `desc`
and skips adding its own. Switched to
`return vim.tbl_extend('force', desc, { desc = '?' })` so the function
returns a clone with the default added. Caller's table is left intact.

body:
## Resolution
The original test had inline `tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)` and inline
`rm -rf "$tmpdir"` at end of test. If any assertion in between failed,
bats aborts the test before the `rm -rf` runs, leaking the tmp dir under
`/tmp`. Extracted into bats `setup()` / `teardown()` (named `TMPDIR_TEST`
to avoid colliding with the standard `TMPDIR` env var). Bats guarantees
`teardown()` runs even on assertion failure.

body:
## Resolution
Original premise wrong. The cascade prefers gum because
`sesh.sh` is invoked via `tmux display-popup -E`, and `fzf-tmux -p`
cannot nest another popup inside the existing one. gum filter renders
inline and is the only rich-ish picker that works in this context.
The "rich UI" the original finding lamented (preview, ctrl-a/t/g/x/f/d
binds) is reachable only by running the script outside a popup — and
the cascade still falls through to fzf-tmux/fzf there. No defect.
Fix history (merged from the duplicate Pass 6 Fixed record, Fixed:
2026-05-05, Reverted: 2026-05-05): the initial fix reordered the
cascade to fzf-tmux → fzf → gum → select, which broke the picker
entirely — by the time `sesh.sh` runs it is already inside a tmux
popup (`prefix + t` via `tmux display-popup -E`,
`config/tmux/tmux.conf:96`) and `fzf-tmux -p` cannot open a second
popup. The cascade was reverted and an explanatory comment block was
added at the top of `sesh.sh` documenting the popup-nesting
constraint.

Migrated from v1 `nitpicker-findings.md` (Pass 10, 2026-05-18).
title: Picker cascade prefers gum over fzf-tmux, losing rich UI

body:
## Problem

Six command substitutions in the test suite use the BSD-first fallback
`$(stat -f FMT FILE 2>/dev/null || stat -c FMT FILE)`. On GNU coreutils,
`stat -f` means "filesystem status", so the BSD format string is parsed
as a *file operand*: stat errors on `%Lp`/`%m` (exit 1, triggering the
fallback) but **still prints a multi-line filesystem-status block for the
real file to stdout**. The substitution captures that block plus the
fallback's value, so the compared string is never `600`/`700`/a bare
mtime.

Sites: `tests/dfm.bats:319,478,495,497` and `tests/theme-lib.bats:47,50`.

## Evidence

`bats tests/` at HEAD on Linux 6.8: 4 failures —
`not ok 31 dfm secrets create writes files readable only by owner`
(`[ "$perms" = "600" ]` failed), `not ok 48 ... directories owner-only`
(`[ "$perms" = "700" ]` failed), the re-tighten test at dfm.bats:499
(`[ "$fperms" = "600" ]` failed), and theme-lib.bats:51
(`[ "$before" = "$after" ]` failed — the captured blocks embed live
`Free:` block counts that change between the two calls).

Empirical demo on this host:
`perms="$(stat -f '%Lp' f 2>/dev/null || stat -c '%a' f)"` yields
`File: "..." / ID: ... / Block size: 4096 / ... / 600` (262 bytes of
filesystem status + the real value).

The production code under test is correct: `local/bin/dfm-lib:243-265`
sets umask 077 and explicitly chmods 700/600.

## Impact

The suite (`test-all.sh`, the package.json test script) is
deterministically red on every Linux host, and the failing assertions are
exactly the ones verifying the security-critical secrets-permission
behavior — a real permission regression on Linux would be
indistinguishable from the known-red state.

## Fix

Swap each substitution to GNU-first; BSD stat rejects `-c` with an error
on stderr and no stdout, so the ordering is safe on macOS:
`perms="$(stat -c '%a' FILE 2>/dev/null || stat -f '%Lp' FILE)"`
(and `stat -c %Y ... 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m ...` in theme-lib.bats).
This mirrors the already-correct probe pattern in
`local/bin/tv-gh-repos:86-95` (`cache_file_mtime`).

## Resolution
Swapped all six substitutions GNU-first (stat -c ... || stat -f ...) in tests/dfm.bats and tests/theme-lib.bats; full suite 189/189 green on Linux 6.8.
title: BSD-first stat fallback breaks 4 bats tests on GNU/Linux

body:
## Problem

`config/fish/conf.d/theme-switch.fish:35,46` capture the mode-file mtime
with the BSD-first fallback
`(stat -f %m $mode_file 2>/dev/null; or stat -c %Y $mode_file 2>/dev/null)`.
On GNU/Linux, `stat -f %m FILE` exits 1 (so `or` runs the fallback) but
first prints a multi-line filesystem-status block for `$mode_file` to
stdout. The captured variable is therefore a multi-element fish list:
filesystem-status lines (including live `Free:` block counts) plus the
real mtime.

## Evidence

Empirically verified on this host (Linux 6.8, GNU coreutils):
`stat -f %m FILE 2>/dev/null` → exit 1, 262 bytes of filesystem status on
stdout. The same defect makes `tests/theme-lib.bats:51` fail, proving the
`Free:` counts change between two captures ~1s apart.

The per-prompt watcher at line 46 compares
`test "$__theme_switch_last_mtime" != "$mtime"`; both sides embed
free-block counts, so any disk write between two prompts makes the
strings differ even though the mode file's mtime is unchanged.

## Impact

On Linux, the "cheap watcher" trips its expensive branch on effectively
every prompt after any disk activity: `echo y | fish_config theme save
"Catppuccin Mocha"` (subprocess + theme-file write), ls-colors re-read,
and BAT_THEME reset run per prompt instead of only on real theme flips.
Adds per-prompt latency and constant writes to fish's theme file.

## Fix

Swap both substitutions to GNU-first (BSD stat rejects `-c` cleanly with
no stdout):
`(stat -c %Y $mode_file 2>/dev/null; or stat -f %m $mode_file 2>/dev/null)`
at lines 35 and 46.

## Resolution
GNU-first stat order in theme-switch.fish lines 35/46; fish -n and fish_indent --check pass.
title: theme-switch.fish mtime capture polluted on Linux; watcher trips every prompt

body:
## Problem

The three phpenv fish files committed in f89010c
(`chore(deps): update phpenv.fish`) do not pass `fish_indent --check`:
`config/fish/functions/phpenv.fish`, `config/fish/conf.d/phpenv.fish`,
`config/fish/completions/phpenv.fish`. `pre-commit run --all-files` at
HEAD fails on the "Fish indent check" hook on a clean checkout.

## Evidence

`pre-commit run --all-files`: every hook passes except
`Fish indent check....Failed`. Direct per-file check:
`fish_indent --check <file>` fails for exactly the three phpenv files and
no other tracked `.fish` file. The files are synced from the upstream
`ivuorinen/phpenv.fish` plugin; repo history shows the established
convention of reformatting after sync (70cf8cc
`style(fish): reformat phpenv files with fish_indent`), which the latest
sync skipped. CI did not catch it because MegaLinter (run via the
`pr-lint` action on PRs only) has no fish linter and f89010c was pushed
directly to main.

## Impact

The repo's primary quality gate is red at HEAD: any contributor (or
agent Stop-hook flow) running pre-commit gets a failure unrelated to
their change, and the fish_indent pre-commit hook will block the next
commit that happens to touch any fish file path until these files are
reformatted.

## Fix

Run `fish_indent -w config/fish/functions/phpenv.fish
config/fish/conf.d/phpenv.fish config/fish/completions/phpenv.fish` and
commit (matching the 70cf8cc precedent). Longer term, apply the same
formatting in the upstream ivuorinen/phpenv.fish repo so each sync lands
clean.

## Resolution
fish_indent -w applied to the three phpenv fish files (70cf8cc precedent); pre-commit Fish indent check green.
title: phpenv fish files at HEAD fail fish_indent; pre-commit gate red on clean checkout

body:
## Problem

No CI path validates fish files. The only lint workflow
(`.github/workflows/linters.yml`, job "PR Lint") runs MegaLinter via the
`ivuorinen/actions/pr-lint` composite — and MegaLinter ships no fish
linter — and it triggers only on pull requests, so direct pushes to main
receive no lint at all. The local pre-commit config does gate fish
(`Fish syntax check`, `Fish indent check`), creating a local/CI gate
asymmetry.

## Evidence

`grep -nE 'fish|pre-commit' .github/workflows/linters.yml` → no matches;
the workflow's steps are checkout, yarn-lock-changes, and pr-lint
(MegaLinter). Proof of consequence: f89010c landed three phpenv fish
files on main that fail `fish_indent --check` (see the companion
fish-indent finding), with all CI green.

## Impact

Any fish change pushed to main (or any file class MegaLinter does not
cover) bypasses the repo's declared quality bar; HEAD is currently red
against its own pre-commit gate and no CI signal exists to surface it.

## Fix

Add a lightweight workflow (or a step in linters.yml with an
`on: push: branches: [main]` trigger) that installs fish and runs
`fish -n` plus `fish_indent --check` over `git ls-files '*.fish'` —
or simply runs `pre-commit run --all-files`, which already encodes the
full gate.

## Resolution
Added .github/workflows/tests.yml fish-lint job (fish -n + fish_indent --check over git ls-files *.fish) on push to main and PRs.
title: No CI coverage for fish files; direct pushes to main skip lint entirely

body:
## Problem

The bats test suite (33 files under `tests/`, ~189 tests, runner
`test-all.sh` wired as the package.json test script) is not executed by
any GitHub workflow. Workflows present: copilot-setup-steps, linters
(MegaLinter on PRs), new-release, pre-commit-autoupdate, semantic-pr,
sync-labels, update-submodules — none run bats.

## Evidence

`ls .github/workflows/` + per-workflow step inspection: no job invokes
bats, test-all.sh, or the package.json test script. Consequence observed
this run: 4 tests fail at HEAD on Linux (the stat-fallback finding) and
nothing surfaced it — the suite has been red on Linux without any signal.

## Impact

Test regressions land silently. The suite currently verifies
security-relevant behavior (dfm secrets permissions, theme-lib symlink
guards); without CI execution those guarantees only hold on whichever
machine last ran the suite manually — and evidently not on Linux.

## Fix

Add a `tests` workflow running `bats tests/` on push/PR with an
`ubuntu-latest` + `macos-latest` matrix (the matrix alone would have
caught the GNU/BSD stat divergence in both directions). Bats is already
managed via mise, so `jdx/mise-action` + `bats tests/` is sufficient.

## Resolution
Added .github/workflows/tests.yml bats job with ubuntu-latest + macos-latest matrix; actionlint/yamllint/zizmor/prettier clean.
title: bats test suite is not executed by any CI workflow

body:
## Problem

The context-mode sandbox tools (`ctx_execute`, `ctx_execute_file`,
`ctx_batch_execute`) execute arbitrary code with full write access to the
working tree, bypassing the entire file-protection hook chain:
`pre-edit-block.sh` matches only `Edit|Write|Read|MultiEdit`, and the
PostToolUse format/validate hooks likewise never fire for sandbox-side
writes. `.claude/rules/bash-routing.md` declares
"`ctx_execute`/`ctx_execute_file` to write files — use `Write`/`Edit`" a
forbidden pattern, but nothing enforces it.

## Evidence

Verified in this session: a Python `open(path, 'w')` inside `ctx_execute`
created a file in the repo tree (removed immediately after), and
`open('local/bin/fzf-tmux', 'r+')` — a file `pre-edit-block.sh` exists to
protect — succeeded. Settings matcher dump:
`Edit|Write|Read|MultiEdit -> pre-edit-block.sh`, `Bash ->
pre-bash-route.sh`; no matcher covers any `mcp__plugin_context-mode_*`
tool.

## Impact

An agent (or a prompt-injected instruction) can modify hook-protected
paths — `local/bin/fzf-tmux`, `yarn.lock`, `.yarn/`, submodule paths,
real `secrets.d` files — and land unformatted/unvalidated edits, without
any deny, warning, or auto-format firing. The written rule exists; the
enforcement surface has a hole the project's own routing rules actively
push traffic toward.

## Fix

Add a PreToolUse hook entry matching the context-mode execute tools
(matcher `mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute|...ctx_execute_file|...ctx_batch_execute`)
that scans the `code`/`commands` input for write patterns targeting
protected paths (the same list `pre-edit-block.sh` guards) and denies
with the pre-edit-block message. Coarse but sufficient: the protected-path
list is short and literal; matching the path strings in the submitted
code catches the realistic bypass shapes.

## Resolution
Added .claude/hooks/pre-ctx-write-guard.sh + PreToolUse matcher for the three ctx execute tools; five self-test payloads behave correctly (deny writes to protected paths and secrets reads, pass benign/example/null-redirect).
title: context-mode sandbox writes bypass pre-edit-block and all post-edit hooks

body:
## Problem

`package.json` declares `"license": "MIT"` but no license text exists
anywhere in the repository: no `LICENSE`, `LICENSE.md`, or `COPYING` at
the root or under `.github/`, and `README.md` never mentions a license.

## Evidence

`ls LICENSE* COPYING* .github/LICENSE*` → nothing.
`grep -rn license README.md` → nothing.
`package.json` line: `"license": "MIT"`.

## Impact

The MIT license's only substantive condition is that its text accompany
the software; declaring MIT without the text leaves the grant legally
ambiguous, and GitHub's license detection shows the repo as unlicensed —
reusers of the widely-copied helper scripts in `local/bin/` have no
effective permission grant.

## Fix

Add a standard `LICENSE` file at the repo root with the MIT text and the
copyright line (`Copyright (c) Ismo Vuorinen`), matching the package.json
declaration.

## Resolution
Added LICENSE with MIT text, Copyright (c) 2022 Ismo Vuorinen (first-commit year).
title: MIT declared in package.json but no LICENSE file exists

## Problem

The only two npm devDependencies, `typescript` (^6.0.3) and
`@types/node`, have no consumer in the repository.

## Evidence

Tracked JS/TS surface is two plain-JS files
(`.github/tag-changelog-config.js`, `config/amethyst/layouts/almost-maximize.js`),
linted by biome. There is no `tsconfig.json`/`jsconfig.json`, no tracked
`.ts` file, and no script or hook invoking `tsc`
(`git ls-files '*.ts'` → empty; grep for `typescript|tsc` across
package.json scripts and `.claude/hooks/` → only the dependency
declaration itself). All lint CLIs come from mise
(`config/mise/config.toml:94-99`), not from node_modules.

## Impact

Dead weight in every `yarn install` and in Renovate's update stream; the
lockfile churns for packages nothing uses.

## Fix

`yarn remove typescript @types/node`. If they were added for editor
IntelliSense on the two JS files, a `// @ts-check` pragma plus editor-global
TypeScript serves that without a repo dependency.

## Resolution
yarn remove typescript @types/node; lockfile updated via yarn, yarn lint green.
title: Unused devDependencies: typescript and @types/node have no consumer

body:
## Problem

After the v1→v2 findings migration, the v1-format enforcement surface
remained: `.claude/hooks/post-edit-audit-findings-lint.sh` (validates the
retired `docs/audit/*-findings.md` heading hierarchy and regenerates the
deleted `.claude/nitpicker-status.json` sidecar) and
`.claude/rules/audit-findings-structure.md` (documents that format), plus
the hook's PostToolUse registration in `.claude/settings.json`.

## Evidence

`git ls-files 'docs/audit/*-findings.md'` matches nothing since commit
2be15fc; the hook's path filter (`*/docs/audit/*-findings.md`) can never
fire again, and the rule mandates a header format (`# Nitpicker
Findings`, `Last pass: N (date)`) for files that no longer exist. The v2
store is validated by the nitpicker skill's own `findings.py validate`.

## Impact

Dead enforcement: a rule file agents still load and could try to apply to
the v2 store (whose layout it contradicts), and a registered hook that
costs a process spawn on every Edit/Write while guarding nothing.

## Fix

Delete the hook and the rule, and drop the hook's entry from
`.claude/settings.json` PostToolUse.

## Resolution
Deleted post-edit-audit-findings-lint.sh and audit-findings-structure.md, removed the hook's PostToolUse entry from settings.json.
title: Stale v1 findings-format hook and rule survive the v2 migration

## Problem

The forkless upward walk (`d=${d%/*}`) goes from `/x` to the empty string, so `/.git` is never checked and a repository rooted at `/` never gets `--git`.

## Evidence

Trace of the loop: `d=/x` → test `/x/.git` → `d=""` → loop exits; `/.git` untested.

## Impact

Cosmetic only — eza omits git annotations in the pathological repo-at-root case; the script's own `ponytail:` comment already declares the walk's known ceiling.

## Fix

If it ever matters, treat the empty remainder as `/` for one final check before breaking; not worth the branch today.

## Resolution
Added the one missing check: after the walk empties d, test /.git once (local/bin/x-eza-git). shellcheck/shfmt clean, x-eza-git.bats 3/3.
title: x-eza-git upward .git walk never tests the filesystem root

## Problem

grype reports 159 dependency CVEs, all in vendored, cached, or
gitignored paths the repo does not author or version-control.

## Evidence

All matches resolve under `config/vim/extra/fzf/**`,
`config/vim/plugged/**`, or `node_modules/**` — none of which is
tracked by git. Updates flow through the upstream tool chain
(yarn, vim-plug, fzf submodule sync).

## Impact

Informational only — vendored CVE exposure the repo does not
own. Accepted by maintainer ruling on 2026-05-05 (v1 status:
Accepted, informational — no repo-side fix).

## Fix

No repo-side fix. Re-running grype after a `yarn install` /
`vim +PlugUpdate` sweep will refresh the report.

## Resolution
Added .grype.yaml excluding untracked/vendored trees (config/vim/extra, config/vim/plugged, node_modules, tools, cheatsheets); grype dir:. now reports 'No vulnerabilities found', so future scans are signal, not noise.
title: grype reports 159 dependency CVEs in vendored / cached / gitignored paths

body:
## Problem

Heavy SAST tools were not run in pass 1; coverage is deferred.

## Evidence

Tool-coverage record, pass 1 (2026-05-05): semgrep, opengrep,
snyk, checkov available but not run.

## Impact

Whole-tree SAST on a dotfiles repo (heavy on shell/lua/fish/toml)
is high-noise without scoping; deferral accepted by maintainer ruling
on 2026-05-05 (v1 status: Accepted, informational).

## Fix

Run a future security-auditor pass with tighter rule sets
configured; checkov on `.github/workflows/` is also pending.

## Resolution
Scoped SAST delivered: checkov on .github/workflows (200 passed, 0 failed) and semgrep p/default over local/bin, scripts, config/theme (1 finding: ifs-tampering on tv-gh-repos:28 — triaged as intentional strict-mode idiom IFS=$'\n\t', not a defect). snyk/opengrep not pursued: semgrep+checkov cover the SAST surface this repo has.
title: Heavy SAST tools (semgrep, opengrep, snyk, checkov) deferred
